rulings.Case Title: WEISSBERG VS PEINADO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rents [Ccp 708.510] And Appointment Of Receiver [Ccp 708.620 Et Seq.]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2024, Line 13. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WEISSBERG's Motion For Assignment Of Rents [Ccp 708.510] And Appointment Of Receiver [Ccp 708.620 Et Seq.]. Plaintiff's motion for assignment of rents and appointment of receiver is continued to September 12 by which time it may well be moot. (Opp. 4:7-9; Peinado Dec. 2:27-3:2; Rply. 10:8-10.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY LLC VS. KWESI BUABENG

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER MORGAN STANLEY SMITH BARNEY LLC's MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. UNITED EDUCATORS OF SAN FRANCISCO, AFT/CFT, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To (1) Dismiss Respondent David Zeeman, (2) Deny Petition To Vacate, And (3) Confirm Arbitration Award; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2024, Line 2. 1 - RESPONDENT UNITED EDUCATORS OF SAN FRANCISCO, AFT/CFT, AFL-CIO, CT/NEA's Motion To (1) Dismiss Respondent David Zeeman, (2) Deny Petition To Vacate, And (3) Confirm Arbitration Award. United Educators of San Francisco's ("Union") motion to dismiss/confirm is granted as follows: The court denies San Francisco Unified School District's ("SFUSD") petition to vacate or correct the arbitration award and confirms the award pursuant to CCP 1285.2 [response to petition to vacate may request confirmation]. Union's motion to dismiss is denied as it appears that Mr. Zeeman (grievant) is a person bound by the arbitration award within the meaning of CCP 1285. In any event, as explained below, the award is confirmed in his and the Union's favor. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: DAJANI CONSTRUCTION INC., VS. AZALINA EUSOPE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2024, Line 11. 2 - CROSS DEFENDANT DAJANI CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION's DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Off calendar. The Carton declaration suggests cross-complainant is unresponsive to moving counsel's meet-and-confer efforts. Cross-complainant is ordered to comply with the Code. Responsive pleadings are now due by September 9, 2024. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, August 7, 2024, Line 12. DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration.
